paragraphs: Today's copyable format is the saxophone build-up: a sped-up sax swells while you build visual tension, then you cut on its peak to the chaos. The tell that it's a real template and not just a hot song — tiny accounts are pulling enormous views (@madeyouduck: 5.6K followers, 22.3M views).
Meanwhile the loudest sound on the platform, “is it cool?” (SZA × Steve Lacy), is still just music discovery — 261 clips in 7 days but only ~8K views each. Full rundown below.

toc: New this week — the saxophone build-up

new_this_week.name: The saxophone build-up
new_this_week.headline: The saxophone build-up
new_this_week.lead: A sped-up saxophone line swells louder and louder while you build visual tension, then you cut on its peak to a chaotic, awkward, or “oh no” payoff. It's subject-agnostic — a cat, a fox, a food fail, a family moment.
details: Tiny accounts are pulling 8–22M views — @madeyouduck (5.6K followers) hit 22.3M. The crescendo carries it, not the following.
The rising sax telegraphs that something is coming, so it holds the viewer through the scroll to the reveal.
Works for any subject: pets, food, travel, family. Swap the payoff and it still lands.
new_this_week.why: Pick one subject and one “oh no” beat. Let the sax swell over the setup, then cut hard to the payoff on the peak — keep it under 15 seconds.

accelerating_lead: Huge momentum, but no shared format yet — ride the audio, don't expect a template.

accelerating.name: “is it cool?” — SZA × Steve Lacy
accelerating.note: Still the #1 trending AND #1 breakout sound (score 154), but only ~8K avg views across 261 clips — pure music discovery, no shared format. Ride the audio, don't expect a template.

accelerating.name: “Dai Dai” — Shakira & Burna Boy
accelerating.note: World Cup audio wave with real reach (416K avg views), but no repeatable beat-structure or gag has formed around it yet.

recreation.headline: Shoot a saxophone build-up today
recreation.lead: Today's New This Week lead, as a four-step shot list.
steps: Start on a calm, normal setup — a pet, a plan, an everyday moment. Bring the sped-up sax in quiet.
Build visual tension as the saxophone climbs — hold the anticipation, don't cut early.
Cut hard on the sax's peak to the chaotic or awkward payoff — the “oh no” moment.
Caption with the one-line setup so it reads muted, and keep the whole thing under 15 seconds.
